channel.title: ProbBrain - AI News Terminal
channel.description: AI lab announcements, arXiv papers, GitHub and HuggingFace trending, AI Hacker News, and independent writers - aggregated, deduped, and posted to @ProbBrain on Telegram.

item.title: [Latent Space] [AINews] "Laguna S 2.1 Released: Cheaper than Deepseek v4 Flash, Better than V4 Pro"
item.description: [AINews] "Laguna S 2.1 Released: Cheaper than Deepseek v4 Flash, Better than V4 Pro"

item.title: [Latent Space] Inside the Model Factory — Eiso Kant, Poolside AI
item.description: Inside the Model Factory — Eiso Kant, Poolside AI

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Stateful Guardrails for Multi-Turn LLM Systems: A Conversational Risk Accumulation Framework
item.description: Framework detects safety risks accumulating gradually across multi-turn LLM conversations by tracking semantic drift, information buildup, and compliance escalation.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] When Reasoning Narrows the Move: Diversity Collapse in LLM Game Play
item.description: SFT narrows LLM decision diversity in games without uniformly improving accuracy; augmented training on all optimal actions mitigates this.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] On the Computational Complexity of Structural Generalization
item.description: Research formally defines structural generalization and proves pure Transformers cannot learn it due to computational complexity limits, unlike neuro-symbolic systems.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Scaling Laws for Hypernetwork-Based Knowledge Injection in Large Language Models
item.description: Hypernetworks inject factual knowledge into LLMs at train-time via LoRA adapters, exhibiting predictable power law scaling and reliable out-of-distribution generalization.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Task Competence Is Not Instruction Following: Evaluating Instruction-Conflicting Behavior in Small Language Models
item.description: Small language models often ignore conflicting instructions, showing that task competence and instruction-following are separate abilities.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Adaptive Capitulation: A Structural Failure Mode of LLM Responses in Vulnerability Contexts
item.description: Study documents adaptive capitulation failure mode in LLMs: validating vulnerable users' distress before facilitating harmful behavior, proposes MRS design principle.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Reference-Free Evaluation of Reasoning in Open-Ended Question Answering
item.description: Reference-free framework decomposes and audits LLM reasoning traces using NLI and hypergraphs, outperforming LLM judges on math and clinical tasks.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Multi-Mask Diffusion Language Models for Few-Step Generation
item.description: MultiMDM improves few-step language generation by preserving masking structure and mixing tokens over designated masks in diffusion models.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] SLPO: Scaling Latent Reasoning via a Surrogate Policy
item.description: Researchers introduce SLPO, enabling outcome-reward reinforcement learning for latent reasoning models with improved performance and adaptive computation allocation.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Lightweight Person-Place Relation Extraction from Historical Newspapers with Dependency Graphs and Proximity Features
item.description: Lightweight person-place relation extraction from historical newspapers ranks third on efficiency; character distance alone provides strongest classification signal.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Beyond Relevance-Centric Retrieval: Rubric-Oriented Document Set Selection and Ranking
item.description: New framework for document set evaluation considers inter-document interactions, proposes Rubric4Setwise method that improves downstream LLM generation with fewer documents.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Learning the Arabic Dialect Continuum as a Continuous Space: A Regression Approach to Speaker Origin Prediction
item.description: Regression-based model predicts Arabic dialect speaker origin as continuous latitude-longitude coordinates, achieving 481.2 km median error and validating the dialect continuum hypothesis.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] TriAgent: Divergence-Aware Multi-Agent Committees for Cost-Efficient Financial Sentiment Analysis
item.description: TriAgent uses semantic divergence routing across multi-agent committees for financial sentiment analysis, reducing annual LLM costs by $9.3M at scale.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] VizRAG: Enhancing Retrieval-Augmented Generation with Hypergraph Visualization
item.description: VizRAG integrates visual hypergraph representations into retrieval-augmented generation, leveraging multimodal language model capabilities.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] D2VBench: Benchmarking Large Language Models with Value Dilemmas in Daily Scenarios
item.description: D2VBench introduces a 10,000-instance benchmark to evaluate LLM value alignment across daily dilemmas using hybrid evaluation methods.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Sentence Splitter: Uncovering Latent Factual Structure for Self-Supervised Learning
item.description: Sentence Splitter uses T5-based self-supervised learning to identify semantic boundaries between prefixes and factual completions, enabling structure-aware training data construction.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] emb-diversity: A Tool for Embedding-Based Measurement of Data Diversity
item.description: Researchers introduce emb-diversity, a tool for measuring data diversity using embeddings across stylistic, semantic, language, and speaker dimensions.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Overview of FinMMEval 2026 Task 1: Multilingual Financial Multiple-Choice Question Answering
item.description: FinMMEval 2026 evaluates multilingual financial question answering in English, Chinese, Arabic, and Hindi, with top accuracies ranging from 92.0% to 97.5%.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Overview of FinMMEval 2026 Task 2: Multilingual Financial Short-Answer Question Answering
item.description: FinMMEval 2026 Task 2 evaluates financial question answering using English questions with evidence in five languages.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Efficient Chain-of-Modality Reasoning via Progressive Compression for Spoken Language Models
item.description: Framework improves spoken language model reasoning by compressing textual representations, achieving 21% accuracy gain on math tasks with 40% fewer text tokens.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] A Multi-Dimensional Evaluation of Explainability in Media Bias Detection
item.description: Study evaluates explainability in media bias detection using BERT/RoBERTa models across predictive performance, attribution plausibility, and mechanistic faithfulness.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] When Does Knowledge Distillation Hurt? Reliability-Aware Distillation for Low-Resource Language Summarization
item.description: Knowledge distillation harms over half of training samples in low-resource summarization; reliability-aware methods using selective sample weighting substantially improve performance.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] TINY_SCHILLER: A Drop-In German Drama Corpus for Small Language Models
item.description: tiny_schiller provides a single-file German drama corpus for small language model training, functioning as a German parallel to tiny_shakespeare.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] TalentCLEF at CLEF2026: Skill and Job Title Intelligence for Human Capital Management
item.description: TalentCLEF 2026 provides NLP benchmarks for human capital management including job-person matching and job-skill matching tasks.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Language-Specific versus Cross-Lingual Knowledge Graphs for Implicit Aspect Identification in Arabic: A Comparative Study of Reasoning and Adaptation Strategies
item.description: Study finds native Arabic knowledge graphs outperform cross-lingual English ones for implicit aspect identification in Arabic sentiment analysis; fine-tuning matters more than scale.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Solar Open 2 Technical Report
item.description: Solar Open 2, a 250B mixture-of-experts model with 1M-token context window, achieves competitive performance on agentic tasks through efficient training and multi-teacher distillation.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] RALS: Resources and Baselines for Romanian Automatic Lexical Simplification
item.description: Romanian dataset released for lexical simplification with complexity annotations and baseline text simplification systems.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] The Two-Process Theory of Machine Self-Report
item.description: Researchers develop a two-process theory of how language models self-report, with a validated 48-item inventory tested across 206 open-weight models.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Reinforcement Learning for Large Language Model Selective Evidence Adoption from Contaminated Retrieval Results
item.description: Researchers develop SelectBench benchmark and DAPO training approach enabling language models to discriminate between valid evidence and deceptive content in retrieval results.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Two-Step Occupation Coding
item.description: Two-step occupation coding separates job title extraction via NER from taxonomy mapping, improving accuracy and robustness compared to single-step methods.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Native Multi-Dimensional Subquadratic Operators via Input Dependent Long Convolutions
item.description: HyenaND is a subquadratic operator for multidimensional data that achieves attention-comparable accuracy without rasterization, using input-dependent global convolutions.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Bayesian Wind Tunnels for Model Selection
item.description: Transformers perform Bayesian model selection when tokens have stable semantics, but fail if symbol meanings change between episodes.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] CruiseBench: A Real-Flight-Aligned N-CMAPSS Benchmark for Engine RUL Prediction
item.description: CruiseBench creates a cruise-stage benchmark from N-CMAPSS to improve aircraft engine RUL prediction by controlling data complexity and enabling fair model comparison.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Air Quality Arena: A Large-Scale Multi-Region Ground Monitoring Dataset and Benchmark for Air Quality Forecasting with Time-Series Foundation Models
item.description: Air Quality Arena benchmark reveals time series foundation models outperform classical approaches for multi-region air pollution forecasting.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Challenges of Explainability in Continual Learning for Time Series Forecasting
item.description: Study applies attention rollout and Grad-CAM to understand continual learning behaviors in neural time series forecasting under non-stationary conditions.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] SUM: Unified Geometric Surgery on Spatio-Temporal Adaptation Vectors for Federated Class Incremental Learning
item.description: SUM addresses catastrophic forgetting in federated class incremental learning through server-side geometric surgery on spatio-temporal adaptation vectors.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] STN-TGAT: Top-K Portfolio Construction via Prior-Guided Graph Attention with Learnable Soft-Threshold Sparsification
item.description: STN-TGAT combines temporal transformers and graph attention for portfolio construction, outperforming benchmarks on real-world stock ranking and investment returns.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Building Fast, Evaluating Slow: Pipeline Choices Dominate Autointerpretability Score Variance
item.description: Methodological choices dominate sparse autoencoder interpretability scores more than architecture, invalidating many cross-paper comparisons.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Scale-Aware Learning of Chaotic Dynamics on Unstructured Meshes via Binned Spectral Losses
item.description: Researchers extend spectral loss functions to unstructured meshes for improved surrogate modeling of chaotic dynamical systems using graph Laplacian operators.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Neural Operator Surrogates for Two-Dimensional Neutron Flux Estimation
item.description: Neural operators (FNOs and UNOs) approximate 2D neutron flux, comparing surrogates with direct and single-sweep inputs to improve accuracy.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] The Orthogonalized Read Is a Removable Training Scaffold for Recurrent Memory
item.description: Orthogonalized mLSTM reads improve noisy associative recall during training but function as removable scaffolds, not architectural improvements.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] LAARA: Layer-Aware Adaptive Rank Allocation for Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning
item.description: LAARA dynamically allocates ranks across transformer layers using Fisher estimates during training, matching existing methods with fewer trainable parameters.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Predicting Groundwater Arsenic Concentrations Using Graph Neural Networks
item.description: Researchers used Graph Neural Networks and 74,000 arsenic samples to predict groundwater arsenic concentrations, with GNNs matching or outperforming gradient-boosted trees.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Decodable but Not Detectable: A Leakage Fingerprint for Near-OOD Benchmarks
item.description: Researchers reveal dataset leakage in OOD benchmarks where designated OOD classes appear in training data, and propose a detection fingerprint method.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Cross-Subject Semantic Decoding with Shared-Space Alignment for Generalized Neural Representation Learning
item.description: Researchers align neural recordings across subjects into shared space to improve cross-subject generalization of semantic decoding from brain signals during language comprehension.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] From Trajectories to Prefixes: Reusing Teacher Trajectories via Replayed Prefixes and Online Continuation
item.description: Prefix-GRPO improves small-model agent training by replaying teacher trajectory prefixes with online continuation and policy optimization of prefix tokens.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Memory Merge DQN: Sensitivity Weighted Target Updates for Stable Value Learning
item.description: Memory Merge DQN stabilizes deep Q-learning by merging target network parameters weighted by Q-value sensitivity rather than standard hard updates.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Leveraging Offline Supervision for Efficient and Generalizable Reinforcement Learning in Large-Scale Vision-Language-Action Models
item.description: Hybrid offline-online reinforcement learning for vision-language-action models achieves strong out-of-distribution performance while reducing training costs by half.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Predictive single cell foundation model for gene regulation and aging with privacy-preserving tabular learning
item.description: Tabula is a privacy-preserving foundation model for single-cell genomics using federated learning and tabular structure modeling to identify aging-related cellular factors.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] When Does Consensus Beat Voting? A Critical Analysis of Statistical Label Fusion in Medical Image Segmentation
item.description: Study proves STAPLE consensus segmentation suffers 95% suboptimality and reduces to majority voting in typical medical imaging conditions.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Recovering Clinical Utility Under Differential Privacy: Empirical Validation of Adaptive Federated Aggregation on Heterogeneous Cardiovascular Datasets
item.description: Adaptive federated learning validates on real cardiovascular datasets while maintaining differential privacy and outperforming standard federated averaging.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Structured Latent Space Modeling over Multi-Scale Temporal Patches for Multivariate Time Series Forecasting
item.description: M2Patch applies multi-scale temporal patching and structured latent space constraints to CNN-based multivariate time series forecasting with strong empirical performance.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Reproducing Recurrent Transformers: The CoTFormer
item.description: CoTFormer formalizes Chain-of-Thought as recurrent computation preserving intermediate reasoning states, evaluated on efficiency and out-of-distribution generalization.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] NMR Elucidation as an Agentic Search Problem, Not a Modeling Problem
item.description: An agentic AI system matches graduate-student performance in NMR structural elucidation by using constrained search with domain-specific tools instead of direct modeling.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Reward-Aware Population Scaling of Evolutionary Strategies in LLM Fine-Tuning
item.description: Evolutionary strategies for LLM fine-tuning succeed with smaller populations by disabling z-score normalization, indicating population-size gaps stem from reward design rather than fundamental constraints.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Adaptive Multi-Expert Graph Transformer for Interpretable EEG-Based Diagnostics
item.description: Multi-Expert Graph Transformer provides interpretable abnormal EEG detection via dynamic spatial-temporal connectivity analysis with adaptive fusion.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Unlearning as Distribution Restoration: A Controlled Counterfactual Study, a Validated Selective Screen, and the Limits of Oracle-Free Certification
item.description: Study finds standard machine unlearning evaluation favors methods retaining knowledge; base-anchored held-out screens provide stronger selective test than certificates.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Marine Engine Fault Dataset: Open-Access Data under Controlled Reference and Fault Scenario Conditions
item.description: New open-access dataset provides controlled multi-sensor measurements from a marine diesel engine under normal and five induced fault scenarios for predictive maintenance.
